description: Review 50+ production traces monthly. Categorize errors using open/axial coding. Identify new failure modes. Feed findings into golden dataset and metric priorities.
Curate a stratified eval dataset of 200+ cases with ground-truth labels. Version-control. Add production failures monthly. Check for contamination and staleness.
Deploy code-based checks (100% of traffic) and LLM-as-judge (sampled traffic). Run on every eval suite execution. Report pass rates by metric and subgroup.
Compare LLM-judge labels to human labels on 50 sampled cases. Compute agreement (Cohen's Kappa). Update judge prompt if agreement drops below 0.6. Track calibration over time.
Slice pass rates by cuisine, restaurant size, and any other relevant segment. Flag subgroups below floor thresholds. Escalate regressions.
Run full eval suite on every prompt, model, or pipeline change. Block merges if pass rate drops below threshold. Include red-team regression probes.
Track input distribution shifts, output consistency, and concept drift signals. Alert when cuisine mix shifts >10% or new categories appear. Alert on judge/human divergence.
Classify system failures against regulatory-incident categories. Notify legal within 24h of potential reportable incident. Maintain incident log with root cause and corrective action.
Write a structured ship/hold/conditional memo for every non-trivial release. Include eval evidence, guardrail status, rollback plan, and sign-offs.
Monthly summary for leadership: what the system gets right, where it fails and business cost, trend vs prior month, asks for next month. No technical jargon.
Track monthly cost of eval infrastructure: LLM-judge API calls, human review hours, dataset annotation. Report cost per 1000 evaluated requests. Flag if cost exceeds budget.
Grade 50 production traces weekly using structured rubric. Compare to LLM-judge output. Feed disagreements into judge recalibration. Feed new failure cases into golden dataset.
